page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARMv9 architecture, this server features 64 physical cores using the AWS Neoverse-V3 model running at 2.8 GHz. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation and a single thread per core. The memory configuration provides 256.0 GB of RAM, equating to 4.0 GB per core, alongside a cache structure consisting of 64 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. Local storage is bundled with 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 34 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the m9gd.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance and memory throughput. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression both register in the top tier, while single-core performance remains in the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests, including cached small block reads at 9194.14 GB/sec and uncached large block writes at 163.23 GB/sec, place the instance in the top tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier results, with Redis reaching 181.85 million operations per second. However, LLM inference speeds for both 135M and 7B models fall into the average tier.

The inclusion of high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides high resource density for data-intensive applications without requiring external storage attachments. This hardware profile is suited for workloads such as database operations, static web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and general-purpose applications requiring high memory bandwidth and local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database processing capabilities, as evidenced by its top-tier Redis SET operations and Passmark database scores. While single-core CPU performance and LLM inference speeds fall into the average tier, its high resource density and bundled local storage offer qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive workloads. The server is suited for database hosting, static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and general-purpose applications requiring high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 64 Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database benchmark performance.

256.0 GB of system memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark results
